channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: CPI do INSS termina como palco de disputa política e falta de relatório vai virar mote de campanha
item.description: The INSS CPI concluded without approving a final report after government allies rejected the draft by deputy Alfredo Gaspar, enabling the opposition to claim the Lula administration shielded the investigation involving the president’s son, while government supporters argue their version reflected the majority and excluded other implicated parties. The commission, meant to investigate pension fraud and mismanagement, devolved into a political battleground, with mutual accusations between Lula and Bolsonaro factions and a failed attempt to implicate STF ministers linked to banker Daniel Vorcaro. The outcome fuels electoral narratives for 2026, as both sides rapidly produce social media content to sway voters.

item.title: Relatório da CPI do INSS é rejeitado; saiba como votaram os parlamentares
item.description: The INSS CPI rejected the final report drafted by Deputy Alfredo Gaspar (PL-AL), which called for the indictment of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), banker Daniel Vorcaro, Senator Weverton Rocha (PDT-MA), Deputy Euclydes Pettersen (Republicanos-MG), Deputy Gorete Pereira (MDB-CE), and 211 others. The vote took place in the early hours of Saturday, the 28th, in Brasília, with participation from deputies and senators.

item.title: CPMI do INSS termina sob inércia investigativa, mas com desgaste político para Lula
item.description: The INSS CPMI concluded without new evidence but inflicted significant political damage on President Lula by linking his son to corruption suspicions, reinforcing Bolsonarist narratives and reviving corruption as Brazilians' top concern — surpassing crime and inflation, per AtlasIntel/Bloomberg. Though hampered by judicial rulings blocking evidence gathering, the commission fulfilled its role as an electoral platform, aiding the opposition on social media and undermining the PT’s ethical discourse ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: CPMI do INSS rejeita relatório que indiciava e pedia prisão de Lulinha
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI) rejected the final report drafted by Deputy Alfredo Gaspar (PL-AL), which called for the arrest of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, known as Lulinha, son of President Lula, and the indictment of 216 individuals. The decision was made in the early hours of today, suggesting a lack of consensus or insufficient evidence to support the allegations.

item.title: CPMI do INSS rejeita relatório que pedia indiciamento de 'Lulinha' e ex-ministros de Lula e Bolsonaro
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI) rejected its final report recommending the indictment of over 200 individuals, including President Lula’s son Fábio Luís Lula da Silva ('Lulinha'), former ministers, and lawmakers. The report was voted down 19-12, leaving the committee without an approved document before its deadline. The committee president stated copies of the report would still be sent to the STF and PGR, despite the rejection.

item.title: Parlamentares denunciam relator da CPI do INSS à PF por estupro
item.description: Federal deputy Lindbergh Farias (PT-RJ) and senator Soraya Thronicke (Podemos-MS) accused Alfredo Gaspar (PL-AL), the rapporteur of the INSS CPI, of rape and attempted bribery involving a 13-year-old minor in Alagoas nine years ago. Gaspar denied the allegations, claiming they refer to a cousin and are false attempts to derail the CPI’s investigations. The incident caused turmoil in the committee, including verbal exchanges and a request to remove an offensive term from the records.

item.title: Caiado x Leite: o que dizem os números da Quaest sobre a disputa nas prévias do PSD
item.description: Governors Ronaldo Caiado (GO) and Eduardo Leite (RS) show similar performance in the first round of the PSD’s 2026 presidential primaries, with 4% and 3% voting intentions, respectively. In a hypothetical second-round runoff against Lula, Caiado performs better with 32% support versus Leite’s 26%, though both trail the incumbent president. Caiado has greater vote potential and an edge on public security, while Leite is considered more moderate.

item.title: Defesa de Bolsonaro pede livre acesso dos filhos à domiciliar
item.description: The defense of Jair Bolsonaro requested unrestricted access for his children to the residence where he is serving house arrest, as ordered by Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes. The request aims to allow his heirs to visit him without restrictions during the enforcement of the precautionary measure.

item.title: 'Sempre vou consultar meu pai, mas o candidato sou eu', diz Flávio
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, the PL's presidential candidate for 2026, stated during his third visit to the U.S. that while he regularly consults his father Jair Bolsonaro, the final decision in the election is his own. He ruled out the possibility that his father, currently under house arrest, would hold any position in his potential administration.

item.title: Zanin suspende eleições indiretas para governador do Rio e mantém presidente do TJ-RJ no cargo
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin suspended the indirect elections for governor of Rio de Janeiro and ordered that the president of the TJ-RJ, Justice Ricardo Couto de Castro, remain in office until the Court rules on the case. The decision was requested as a highlight in ADI 7942, concerning electoral rules in the state, and should be ratified by the full Court, preferably in person.

item.title: STF: Zanin suspende eleição indireta no RJ; caso vai a plenário presencial
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin suspended the indirect election for the temporary gubernatorial mandate in Rio de Janeiro and requested a highlight for the case to be judged in the Court's physical plenary. The decision follows the STF's prior formation of a majority in favor of indirect and secret elections in another process. The judgment will be restarted, with the date to be set by Court President Minister Edson Fachin.

item.title: Zanin suspende eleição indireta para mandato-tampão no RJ
item.description: STF Minister Cristiano Zanin suspended the indirect election for the temporary mandate in Rio de Janeiro, keeping the president of the state’s Court of Justice in office until the final ruling. He also requested that the case on election rules be heard in the physical plenary, jointly with another proceeding, and denounced Claudio Castro’s resignation as a fraudulent maneuver to evade punishment and manipulate the electoral process. The decision responds to a petition by the PSD, Eduardo Paes’s party, which challenged the TSE’s order to make the supplementary election indirect.

item.title: Por que Zanin suspendeu eleição indireta para governador do Rio
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin suspended the indirect election for governor of Rio de Janeiro, keeping Rio’s Court of Justice President Ricardo Couto in office pending final judgment. The decision supports a PSD petition led by Eduardo Paes calling for a direct vote, following Zanin’s withdrawal of his prior vote in favor of indirect election. The case now proceeds to the STF’s physical plenary, as Rio faces a leadership vacuum after Castro’s resignation one day before his TSE conviction.

item.title: Tebet diz que Nunes é “deselegante” ao chamá-la de “marionete de Lula”
item.description: Minister Simone Tebet criticized São Paulo Mayor Ricardo Nunes as 'absolutely inelegant' after he called her a 'Lula puppet' during her PSB affiliation. She said she holds affection for Nunes but found the remark offensive to Brazilian women. Tebet affirmed her respect for President Lula and Vice President Alckmin, stating she is running for Senate in 2026 at their request, after leaving the MDB after 29 years.

item.title: Nova biografia de Lula mantém tom acrítico e tem autor como personagem oculto
item.description: A new biography of Lula is criticized for maintaining an uncritical tone and for having the author as a hidden character in the narrative, raising concerns about its impartiality. The full content of the article could not be accessed due to the site's robots.txt restrictions.

item.title: Zanin suspende eleições indiretas para governador do Rio de Janeiro
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin suspended the indirect election for governor of Rio de Janeiro, keeping the state's Court of Justice president, Ricardo Couto de Castro, in office until an in-person ruling. The decision follows a new PSD action challenging the TSE's authorization for indirect elections, with Zanin advocating for joint discussion with a separate case calling for direct elections. While the Court majority previously voted for a secret indirect ballot, Zanin and three others support direct voting.

item.title: Zanin interrompe julgamento, suspende nova eleição e mantém governador interino no RJ
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Cristiano Zanin withdrew his vote in the case concerning new gubernatorial elections in Rio de Janeiro and issued an injunction suspending the indirect election for the post. The decision keeps the interim governor in office pending further judicial resolution.

item.title: Rodrigo Bacellar é preso novamente: os tentáculos do Comando Vermelho na política do Rio, segundo investigações
item.description: Rodrigo Bacellar, former president of the Rio de Janeiro State Legislature (Alerj), was arrested again in Operation Unha e Carne III on suspicion of leaking confidential information and obstructing investigations tied to criminal organizations in Rio. The arrest followed the annulment of his mandate by the TSE and the loss of privileged jurisdiction, despite his lawyers claiming he had fully complied with all precautionary measures. Bacellar had previously been arrested in December and released after the Alerj revoked the detention.

item.title: O que Moraes disse ao decretar a nova prisão de Rodrigo Bacellar
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes ordered a new preventive detention for former state deputy Rodrigo Bacellar (União) after he lost his parliamentary mandate by TSE ruling, citing the need to maintain public order and evidence of coordinated actions to obstruct investigations. Bacellar was denounced by the PGR for obstructing an investigation involving an armed criminal organization and public agent participation.

item.title: CPMI do INSS quer indiciar ‘Lulinha’ e ex-ministros de Lula e Bolsonaro
item.description: The rapporteur of the INSS CPMI, federal deputy Alfredo Gaspar, requested the indictment and preventive detention of Lulinha, son of President Lula, for traveling to Spain during the investigation, which is interpreted as an attempt to flee. The report also recommends indicting former ministers from both Lula and Bolsonaro administrations over alleged fraud in the INSS.

item.title: “Conselhos” de Lula repercutem e viram alvo de críticas
item.description: During the 18th Federative Caravan in Rio de Janeiro, President Lula gave advice to Brazilians, referencing his age of 80 and pushing back against claims that he is too old to run for a fourth term. He often makes such comments at public events. His remarks sparked backlash and became a target of criticism.

item.title: Janja critica Nikolas Ferreira e defende ‘lei da misoginia’
item.description: First Lady Janja Lula da Silva criticized Deputy Nikolas Ferreira while defending the bill that criminalizes misogynistic speech, approved by the Senate. In an Instagram video, she urged the Chamber of Deputies to vote on the bill quickly, emphasizing the need to protect women from gender-based hatred.

item.title: CPI do INSS: Flávio chama relatório do PT de “tentativa desesperada”
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) criticized the PT's alternative report in the INSS CPMI, calling it a 'desperate attempt' to divert attention and protect President Lula and his son Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha). He claimed the report improperly included him among 201 indicted individuals based on alleged links between his administrator and an investigated businessman, and suggested Lula is the 'boss' of a supposed criminal organization involved in the stolen pensions case.

item.title: BofA recomenda posição comprada em real ante peso mexicano com commodity e carry
item.description: Bank of America Securities recommends a long position in the Brazilian real against the Mexican peso, with a target of 3.8 Mexican pesos and a stop at 3.2, citing favorable carry and the real's strong positive correlation with commodity prices, especially oil. The bank views the real as undervalued and sees Brazil's 2026 elections as presenting a favorable risk-reward asymmetry, where a market-friendly government could significantly improve economic policy, while the status quo is already relatively benign. Key risks include a more conservative shift by Mexico's central bank or political surprises in Brazil before the election.

item.title: PSD aciona STF para que seja determinada eleição direta no Rio
item.description: The PSD has filed a new action with the STF to suspend the TSE's ruling that allows indirect elections in Rio de Janeiro, seeking to establish a direct election in the state. Minister Cristiano Zanin, the appointed rapporteur, previously voted in favor of direct elections in another case. The party argues the measure is essential to ensure the democratic legitimacy of the state election.

item.title: Edilson Damião toma posse como governador de Roraima após renúncia de Denarium
item.description: Edilson Damião, former vice-governor of Roraima, assumed the governorship after Antonio Denarium resigned to run for the Senate in the 2026 elections. Damião, who joined União Brasil in March 2026, announced his intention to run for governor in 2026. Both face a cassation process at the TSE for alleged abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections, with the trial suspended three times, most recently in November 2025 following a request for additional review by Minister André Mendonça.

item.title: Antonio Denarium renuncia ao governo de Roraima para concorrer ao Senado
item.description: Roraima's governor, Antonio Denarium (PP), resigned to run for Senate in the 2026 elections, complying with electoral disaffiliation requirements. His vice-governor, Edilson Damião (União), assumed state leadership. Denarium cited approximately 80% public approval, asserted his innocence in ongoing TSE electoral cases, and stated that voters will ultimately judge his political legacy.

item.title: Leia a íntegra do relatório alternativo do PT na CPI do INSS
item.description: The PT presented an alternative report to the INSS CPI, exceeding 1,800 pages, to obstruct the vote on the official opinion recommending the indictment of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), son of President Lula. Led by Deputy Rogério Correia (PT-MG), the document expands the list of investigated individuals to around 270 and focuses political attacks on Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), aiming to delay approval of the report involving the presidential family.

item.title: Simone Tebet oficializa filiação ao PSB para concorrer ao Senado em São Paulo
item.description: Planning Minister Simone Tebet officially joined the PSB to run for the Senate in São Paulo in the 2026 elections. She will be part of a ticket led by Fernando Haddad (PT), aiming to counter the influence of Tarcísio de Freitas and Flávio Bolsonaro in the country’s largest electoral college. Tebet emphasized her opposition to Bolsonarism and her long history in the MDB, where she remained since 1997 before switching parties.

item.title: PT adia para 4 de maio jantar com Lula para arrecadar fundos
item.description: The Workers' Party (PT) postponed its fundraising dinner to May 4 at 8 p.m. at the CICB in Brasília, featuring President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, who is seeking re-election in 2026. Originally scheduled for April 6, the event now offers only a single donation tier of R$1,000 via PIX, with digital invitations sent after WhatsApp confirmation. The PT states that contributions strengthen its democratic struggle and references the party’s 46th anniversary, completed on February 10.

item.title: Flávio, sobre CPMI: ‘Se há organização criminosa capo está na principal cadeira do Planalto’
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a 2026 presidential pre-candidate, accused President Lula of being the 'boss' of a criminal organization involved in the 'stolen pensions' INSS scheme, claiming Lula hastily sent his son Fábio Luís (Lulinha) abroad to halt CPMI investigations. He called the government's CPMI report a desperate attempt to deflect attention and protect Lula and his son, while the parallel report by rapporteur Alfredo Gaspar (PL-AL) recommended indicting Lulinha, banker Daniel Vorcaro, multiple lawmakers, and over 200 others, exempting only Frei Chico due to lack of evidence of illicit acts.

item.title: PSD e votos no STF embaralham eleições de mandato-tampão no RJ
item.description: The PSD filed an action with the STF requesting that the Rio de Janeiro gubernatorial election be direct, contrary to the TSE's decision for an indirect vote via the State Legislative Assembly. The action follows the resignation of former governor Cláudio Castro (PL) one day before his removal by the TSE for abuse of political and economic power. Justice Alexandre de Moraes advocated for a direct election, gaining support from Gilmar Mendes, Flávio Dino, and Cristiano Zanin, prompting the PSD to file a second action on Friday, March 27.

item.title: Simone Tebet assina filiação ao PSB para concorrer ao Senado por São Paulo
item.description: Planning and Budget Minister Simone Tebet joined the Brazilian Socialist Party (PSB) to run for the Senate from São Paulo, announced at an event in Alesp attended by Geraldo Alckmin and Tabata Amaral. Her departure from the MDB after nearly 30 years was driven by the party's alliance with Governor Tarcísio de Freitas, which blocked her Senate bid. Tebet will run on Fernando Haddad's (PT) gubernatorial ticket, creating internal tension in the PSB as Minister Márcio França also seeks a spot on the ticket.

item.title: Eduardo Leite ganhou força em disputa do PSD?
item.description: The national president of the PSD, Gilberto Kassab, signaled to allies that Governor Eduardo Leite of Rio Grande do Sul is now competing on equal footing for the party's presidential nomination in 2026. The information was reported to Folha by two prominent figures in the party, following a meeting between Kassab and Leite on Wednesday, indicating a strengthening of Leite's position in the internal party race.

item.title: Com indefinição em Minas, plano de Lula pode segurar Silveira no governo
item.description: Minister of Mines and Energy Alexandre Silveira (PSD) may remain in the federal government following signals from President Lula that he intends to retain experienced and politically adept ministers, particularly in Minas Gerais. His stay would ensure continuity in key areas such as energy, critical minerals, and diesel pricing, while strengthening electoral alliances ahead of a potential presidential runoff. Although Silveira has expressed interest in running for the Senate, he conditions his decision on Lula’s approval and the government’s political strategy, with scenarios under discussion including a joint ticket or his succession by Marília Campos (PT) as alternate.

item.title: A estratégia de Moraes ao decretar nova prisão de Bacellar
item.description: Minister Alexandre de Moraes reinstated the preventive detention of former deputy Rodrigo Bacellar (União) after his mandate was annulled by the TSE for abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections. The decision was made ex officio, without a request from the MP or PF, based on obstruction of justice charges filed by the PGR and the need to ensure criminal proceedings and public order. Bacellar's defense called the measure unwarranted and said it will not appeal, stating he had fully complied with all precautionary measures.

item.title: Domiciliar de Bolsonaro tem visitas negadas, PM na porta e Flávio liberado
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro was discharged from hospital and placed under house arrest by order of Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes. Despite being at home, he faces strict restrictions, including denied visits and a police presence outside his residence. His son, Flávio Bolsonaro, has been released from prior restrictive measures.

item.title: Governo Lula exonera Carlos Fávaro do Ministério da Agricultura
item.description: President Lula dismissed Minister of Agriculture Carlos Fávaro on March 27, 2026. Fávaro, a senator from Mato Grosso since 2020, had been appointed minister in 2022 after serving in the government’s transition team. His career includes roles as vice-governor of Mato Grosso and state secretary of Environment.

item.title: Zanin vai relatar ação do PSD que pede eleição direta para mandato-tampão no RJ
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin will be the rapporteur of a PSD action requesting the annulment of the TSE’s decision to hold an indirect election for the interim governorship of Rio de Janeiro. The party claims Cláudio Castro’s resignation was a maneuver to avoid punishment and evade electoral law. Although the STF has already formed a majority for an indirect election, Zanin was part of the minority that advocated for direct elections to uphold constitutional principles and popular sovereignty.

item.title: Aliados de Kassab dizem que Leite equilibrou disputa com Caiado por candidatura do PSD
item.description: The national president of the PSD, Gilberto Kassab, signaled internally to allies that he may support Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite as the party's presidential candidate, balancing the race against Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado for the nomination.

item.title: Otoni de Paula protocola pedido de intervenção federal no Rio
item.description: Federal deputy Otoni de Paula (MDB-RJ) filed a request for federal intervention in Rio de Janeiro with the Federal Public Ministry, citing uncertainty over the succession of former governor Cláudio Castro (PL), who resigned before his trial at the TSE. The move reflects the political and institutional crisis in the state amid the vacancy in the state executive leadership.

item.title: Base do governo pede indiciamento de Bolsonaro na CPMI do INSS
item.description: The government's congressional base presented a report to the INSS CPI proposing the indictment of former President Jair Bolsonaro and Senator Flávio Bolsonaro for allegedly leading a criminal organization that fraudulently manipulated INSS associative discounts. The report calls for the indictment of 130 individuals and refers 71 to the Federal Police for further investigation, totaling 201 names under scrutiny. It denies political motivation, asserting conclusions are based on documentary evidence and individualized conduct.

item.title: De Jair e Flávio Bolsonaro a Vorcaro: os alvos do relatório governista na CPMI do INSS
item.description: The alternative report from Lula’s government base in the INSS CPMI proposes indicting 130 people for alleged involvement in an illegal discount scheme on social security benefits. Targets include Jair Bolsonaro, charged with qualified theft against the elderly, organized crime, and administrative impropriety, and his son Flávio Bolsonaro, indicted for organized crime. Also named are Daniel Vorcaro (Banco Master) and former Labor Minister Onyx Lorenzoni.

item.title: No G7, Vieira e Marco Rubio voltam a falar em cooperação no combate ao crime organizado
item.description: Brazil's Foreign Minister Mauro Vieira and U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio held an unscheduled meeting on the sidelines of the G7 in Paris to discuss a proposed cooperation agreement on combating organized crime, initially put forward by President Lula to former President Trump. The talks emphasized maintaining open diplomatic channels, even as a direct Lula-Trump meeting remains unscheduled, with expectations that it must occur before July due to Brazil entering full election mode. Experts warn that a unilateral U.S. designation of PCC and CV as terrorist groups could inflict severe financial damage on Brazil, potentially exceeding the impact of the Magnitsky Law applied to Minister Alexandre de Moraes.

item.title: Por que Lulinha virou alvo da CPMI do INSS?
item.description: The rapporteur of the INSS CPMI, federal deputy Alfredo Gaspar, proposed the indictment of 216 people, including Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), son of President Lula, for alleged involvement in a billion-dollar fraud scheme involving undue deductions from INSS pensions and retirements. Lulinha is accused of receiving at least R$300 thousand from lobbyist Antônio Camilo, mediated by businesswoman Roberta Luchsinger, linked to the 'Careca do INSS'. The report also names former ministers from Lula and Bolsonaro administrations, lawmakers, and banker Daniel Vorcaro, and awaits a vote by Saturday to be forwarded to the PGR.

item.title: PSD entra com nova ação no STF para que seja determinada a imediata realização de eleições diretas no Rio
item.description: The PSD filed a new action with the STF to overturn the TSE's decision allowing indirect elections in Rio de Janeiro after Cláudio Castro's resignation. The party claims Castro's resignation was an evident fraud to evade punishment and electoral law, demanding immediate direct elections and citing the TSE's opposite ruling in Tocantins in 2018. Justice Cristiano Zanin, who previously supported direct elections in a similar case, is the rapporteur.

item.title: Tereza Cristina diz estar preparada para ser vice na chapa de Flávio
item.description: Senator Tereza Cristina (PP-MS) stated she is prepared to be vice president on Flávio Bolsonaro’s (PL-RJ) 2026 ticket, though she stressed the decision is not hers to make. She also said she could run for president as a woman, criticized sexism in politics, and affirmed that Flávio Bolsonaro is the most competitive candidate to face Lula in 2026.

item.title: Nísia Trindade se filia ao PT e concorrerá à Câmara pelo Rio
item.description: Former Health Minister Nísia Trindade has joined the Workers' Party (PT) and will run for the Chamber of Deputies from Rio de Janeiro. Her campaign will focus on defending science and vaccination, highlighting her leadership at Fiocruz during the Covid-19 pandemic. She left office in February 2025 as part of a ministerial reshuffle in the Lula government.

item.title: Aliado de Castro promete ser candidato caso STF decida por eleição direta
item.description: State deputy Douglas Ruas (PL) stated he would run for governor of Rio de Janeiro if the STF decides on an early direct election. Supported by former governor Cláudio Castro (PL), Ruas is already a pre-candidate of his party for the state race.

item.title: Pimenta fala em “conexão direta” entre fraude do INSS e “BolsoMaster”
item.description: Deputy Paulo Pimenta (PT-RS) stated there is a 'direct connection' between INSS fraud, the Banco Master case, and Jair Bolsonaro's government, dubbing the scandal 'BolsoMaster'. He advocates for Bolsonaro's indictment on charges of organized crime, qualified theft against the elderly, and administrative impropriety, based on an alternative CPMI-INSS report calling for the indictment of 130 individuals, including the former president and his son, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro.
